title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Browser and Node Client Systems · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: A browser support engineer tracing a cross-runtime client incident must isolate a plausible but incorrect result from conflicting traces, repair its cause, and retain a regression witness for https, tls identity, certificate trust, proxies, and downgrade boundaries, producing transport trust and certificate evidence record. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch transport trust and certificate evidence record and publish the reproduction, evidence, re…
objectives: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Preserve hostname, chain, validity, algorithm, and revocation-policy checks without disabling verification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Add an intended private trust root or test certificate without replacing or weakening unrelated trust. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Https Identity
steps.instruction:
debug case — cross-runtime browser support client connects to api4.internal.test through proxy.example.test using a private CA and optional client certificate. Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Inspect the Https Identity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Https Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind https authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. demonstrate the result through typescript 7 strict types plus whatwg fetch request, response, headers, url, streams, and abortsignal contracts. For Fetch and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Https Identity, keep Https Identity connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Reject unintended HTTP downgrades, insecure redirects, mixed content, and credential forwarding across weaker channels. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

For Fetch and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Downgrade Mixed Content, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A final HTTPS URL makes an earlier plaintext hop harmless. This remains false for TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch.
